name: T-Mobile 600
note: 600 MHz (Band 71) — T-Mobile's nationwide low-band 5G 'Extended Range', won at the 2017 broadcast incentive auction. Low frequencies reach far and punch into buildings.

name: AT&T 700
note: Lower 700 MHz — AT&T's long-reach LTE/5G low band, a sub-block of the wider 700 MHz LTE allocation.

name: Verizon 700
note: Upper 700 MHz C-block (Band 13) — Verizon's original nationwide LTE backbone. This is the 746–757 MHz downlink half; handsets reply on the paired 776–787 MHz uplink. It sits at the top of the commercial 700 MHz band, just below public-safety FirstNet.

name: Dish AWS-4
note: AWS-4 (2 GHz) — Dish Network spectrum behind the Boost Mobile / Dish 5G build-out; a slice at the top of the broader AWS cellular block.

name: AT&T 3.45 GHz
note: 3.45 GHz — auctioned from federal radar in 2022; AT&T and Dish are major holders for mid-band 5G (the low end of the n77 band).

name: Verizon C-band
note: C-band (3.7–3.98 GHz) — cleared from satellite in the 2021 'C-band auction'; Verizon won the largest share for its mid-band 5G Ultra Wideband (within the wider n77 band).
